answer:
The Pumble Model Context Protocol (MCP) server allows AI agents, such as Claude, Gemini, or Cursor, to interact with your Pumble workspace [1][2]. By connecting an MCP-compatible client to a Pumble MCP server, you enable the AI to perform actions like sending messages, managing channels, searching workspace history, and adding reactions [1][3][4].

You can connect by adding the server configuration to your client’s settings file (e.g., `claude_desktop_config.json`) using the provided URL, authentication tokens, and headers [1][5]. Various providers offer these servers, ranging from simple API key implementations to comprehensive SDK-based solutions featuring OAuth2 and real-time event support [3][4][2][6].
